title: Dimension constraints implemented
description: Max 8m x 4m in either orientation. Validation working.

title: Pent roof positioning for attachments
description: Fixed rotation and positioning for all 4 attachment directions (left, right, front, back).

title: Roof height constraints
description: Apex: eaves 800-2400mm, crest 1000-4500mm. Pent: both walls 1000-2400mm. Crest must exceed eaves. Hipped: same as apex (geometry TODO).

title: Door/window size validation
description: Openings can't overlap (50mm min gap), can't exceed wall boundaries (100mm from edges). Smart placement finds free spots. Blocks adding when no space left.

title: Marketing landing page
description: Create a landing page to showcase the configurator.

title: README documentation
description: Write comprehensive README for the GitHub repo.

title: MIT License added
description: Added MIT license to the Parametric-shed2 repo.

title: Decision Map page
description: Interactive flowchart for mapping strategic decisions. Added to project board app.

title: Potting Shed preset
description: Created and saved potting shed preset (2m × 3.6m) with three windows and pent roof.

title: Parametric shelving system
description: Add shelving as a new element type (like doors/windows). Parameters: wall, inside/outside, height, starting position, length. Include in BOM.

title: Browser access for James
description: Set up browser access so James can see and interact with the configurator. Options: (1) Clawdbot browser tool with Chrome extension relay, (2) Puppeteer/Playwright automation, (3) VNC/remote desktop. Would enable: screenshots for README, direct visual testing, debugging UI issues like purlin positioning.

title: Monetization decision mapping
description: Map monetization paths in Decision Map with full arguments and evaluation. Options: Enhanced direct sales, DIY plans, Licensing, Niche builds, Spectrum model.

title: 🔄 Competitor Intelligence (ongoing)
description:
Continuous market awareness and competitor monitoring. This is a parent project — never 'done'. See linked deliverables below.

**Completed deliverables:**
• Initial landscape analysis (card #31)
• Tech gap analysis (card #32)
• Quick Garden profile (card #33)

**Files:**
• /competitor-analysis.md (main report)
• /shed-project-board/competitors.md (profiles)

**Backlog:**
• Deep-dive: Crane Garden Buildings
• Deep-dive: Scotts of Thrapston
• Keyword/SEO competitor research
• Pricing intelligence
• Quarterly refresh

title: Add screenshot to README
description: Grab a nice screenshot or GIF of the configurator for the README hero image.

title: SEO improvements
description:
Improve SEO on bespokeshedcompany.co.uk. Full analysis: /home/ser/clawd/memory/seo-analysis-bespokeshedcompany.md

**Critical issues:**
• Thin content (~100 words per product page, need 500+)
• Poor page titles (numbered, generic)
• No blog content since 2019
• Weak location targeting (one mention of Wiltshire)
• Zero reviews showing
• Configurator not mentioned anywhere

**Quick wins:**
1. Fix page titles (remove numbers, add keywords + location)
2. Write 500+ words per product page
3. Get 5 Google reviews
4. Add 'Design Your Own' page linking to configurator

**Medium term:**
• Location landing pages (/garden-buildings-salisbury/ etc)
• Blog with case studies
• FAQ schema markup

title: Website rebuild strategy
description: Decide on approach: refresh current, full rebuild, add e-commerce/quote system, or hybrid configurator model. See Decision Map.

title: Mobile UX for configurator
description: Bottom drawer panel, 55vh height, larger fonts (14-16px), touch-friendly controls, auto-collapse on load, tap-to-close, modern IKEA-inspired theme.

title: Monetization strategy document
description: Create comprehensive monetization strategy doc in the repo. Cover: direct sales, DIY plans, licensing, niche builds, spectrum model, supplier partnerships.

title: Fastest-to-revenue roadmap
description: Prioritize monetization paths by speed-to-revenue. Create actionable roadmap: Week 1, Month 1, Quarter 1 milestones.

title: Embed configurator on bespokeshedcompany.co.uk
description: Deploy parametric configurator on the business website. First monetization step: capture leads, enable quotes.

title: Hipped roof implementation
description: Add hipped roof style option. Currently placeholder in roof style dropdown. Requires new geometry: hip rafters from corners to ridge, jack rafters on all four sides.

title: Fix apex roof on attached buildings
description: Apex roof on attached buildings not working properly. Needs investigation and fix.

title: Wall insulation + plywood lining
description: 50mm PIR insulation between studs, 12mm plywood lining inside. Skips door/window openings. Tied to insulated variant. Added to BOM.

title: Cripple studs below windows
description: Added missing cripple studs from bottom plate to window sill. Works for all wall orientations.

title: Divider height modes
description: Added heightMode: 'walls' (to eaves) or 'roof' (fill to crest). Z-axis dividers get triangular gable frames, X-axis get flat fill.

title: CSG boolean cuts for plywood around openings
description: Plywood panels now use CSG subtraction to cut around doors/windows instead of skipping walls with openings.

title: Fix apex purlins - horizontal positioning
description: Purlins should be HORIZONTAL (not tilted with slope) and SIT ON TOP of rafters. Currently broken - parenting to roofRoot causes unexpected tilt. Needs investigation into transform hierarchy.

title: Code documentation + JSDoc comments
description: Add proper JSDoc comments to roof.js, walls.js, attachments.js. Clean up dead code, improve console logging. PROGRESS: roof.js done (126 lines added), walls.js partial (45 lines), attachments.js partial (64 lines).

title: Hard Rules page
description: Added Rules page to project board with full CRUD. View, add, edit, delete rules. Pre-loaded with working guidelines. Data in data/rules.json.

title: 2D Brain view
description: Added 2D force-directed graph view as alternative to 3D brain. Uses D3.js. Pan/zoom, click nodes for details, drag to rearrange. Shared data file (data/brain.json) keeps 2D and 3D in sync.

title: 📊 Competitor: Landscape analysis
description:
Initial UK market landscape. Three-tier breakdown: Budget (BillyOh, Tiger), Premium Prefab (Quick Garden, Crane, Scotts), True Bespoke. Market positioning identified.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)
**File:** /competitor-analysis.md

title: 📊 Competitor: Tech gap analysis
description:
Comparison table: 3D visualisation, parametric model, real-time pricing, cutting lists. Key finding: NO UK competitor has true parametric 3D with cutting list output. Major differentiator.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)
**File:** /competitor-analysis.md

title: 📊 Competitor: Quick Garden profile
description:
Deep-dive on Quick Garden. European manufacturer, 26k+ customers, log cabin specialists. No online configurator — manual bespoke process. Lead times 4-10 weeks. Pricing examples gathered.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)
**File:** /shed-project-board/competitors.md

title: 📊 Competitor: Crane deep-dive
description:
Full profile of Crane Garden Buildings. Norfolk workshop, 50+ years, 13 UK showrooms, National Trust partnership. Confirmed: their 'configurator' is just an options selector, NOT parametric 3D. Key weakness identified.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)
**File:** /competitor-crane-deep-dive.md

title: 📊 Competitor: Scotts deep-dive
description:
Full profile of Scotts of Thrapston. 100+ years, hardwood specialists, equestrian/garages. Very traditional consultative model. Different customer journey but worth understanding.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)

title: 📊 Competitor: SEO/keyword research
description:
Research what keywords competitors rank for. Identify gaps and opportunities. Tools: Ubersuggest, SEMrush free tier, or manual Google research.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)

title: 📊 Competitor: Pricing intelligence
description:
Gather real pricing data across competitors. Build comparison matrix. Understand where Andrew sits in the market and identify pricing strategy opportunities.

**Parent:** Competitor Intelligence (#13)

title: Avon View Garden Room preset
description: Customer enquiry preset. 7'8" × 9'6" (2340 × 2900mm), apex roof 2m eaves → 2.4m ridge. Door centered on front + 2 narrow windows flanking, picture window on left side.

title: URL state sharing
description: Configs can be shared via URL: ?profile=viewer&c=<base64-encoded-state>. Profile=viewer required to parse URL state. Enables sharing designs with customers.

title: Project board PWA support
description: Mobile-friendly progressive web app. Installable on phone home screen. Touch-friendly interface.

title: Studs showing through plywood (z-fighting)
description: Front wall exterior - studs appeared to show through plywood. Fixed by adjusting geometry/face orientation.

title: Customer photography & case studies
description: Photograph past builds for website and marketing. Create case study pages with before/after, specs, customer quotes. Identified as gap vs competitors (Crane has extensive gallery).

title: Google Reviews campaign
description: Get 5+ Google reviews from past customers. Major SEO quick win — currently showing zero reviews on all product pages. Ask satisfied customers for reviews.

title: STT/voice input for James
description: Set up speech-to-text so James can receive voice messages directly. Needs OpenAI API key (Whisper). Would enable true voice conversations.

title: Roof insulation
description: Add insulation layer to roof (between rafters). Sloped panels (L+R) from eaves to raised tie beam level, horizontal panel between tie beams. Fully parametric - adapts to truss count and building size. Only active for tieBeam: 'raised' configuration. Uses vis.roofIns visibility flag.

title: Floor framing system
description: Add floor joists/framing to configurator if not already present. Include in BOM. Consider bearer/joist options.

title: Context management alerts
description: Proactive alerts before context compaction. James should warn Andrew when context is getting full so important info can be saved.

title: Planning permission guide (blog)
description: Blog post: 'Garden Building Planning Permission Guide'. Common customer concern. Good for SEO, builds trust, answers FAQs.

title: "Why Parametric?" explainer page
description: Website page explaining the parametric difference. Why mm-precision matters, what cutting lists mean, how it's different from competitors' configurators.

title: Attached building frame penetrating primary plywood
description: BUG: Geometry from attached building frame is showing through the plywood of the primary building. Needs CSG subtraction or geometry adjustment to resolve overlap.

title: Divider UI improvements (mobile)
description: Divider controls don't present well, especially on mobile. Review and improve the UI/UX for adding and configuring dividers. Make touch-friendly and visually clearer.

title: 3D camera controls too fast/sensitive
description: User feedback: 3D model navigation is too fast and hard to control. Need to dampen camera movement, slow down rotation/zoom, add smoothing/inertia. Make it much easier for non-technical users to navigate the model.

title: Doors/openings on attachments and dividers
description:
Add ability to place doors, windows, and openings on:
• Attachment buildings (currently only primary building supports openings)
• Internal dividers (for doorways between rooms)

Would enable proper multi-room layouts with access between spaces.

title: More cladding options
description: Add variety of cladding types: shiplap, featheredge, T&G, board-on-board, composite, etc. Visual appearance + material specs for BOM.

title: More window options
description: Expand window types: fixed, opening (casement, top-hung), Georgian style, circular/porthole, etc. Different frame styles and sizes.

title: Window openers/hardware
description: Add opening mechanisms to windows: stays, handles, locks. Include in BOM. Visual indication of which windows open.

title: Stable door option
description: Add stable door (split horizontally, top/bottom open independently). Popular for potting sheds, workshops, stables.

title: Half doors
description: Add half-height door option. Useful for certain applications.

title: Skids for field shelters
description: Add skid runners to base for field shelters. Makes them moveable. Include in BOM. Toggle option for mobile vs fixed.

title: Pillars/posts
description: Add ability to place structural pillars/posts. Useful for carports, covered areas, verandas.

title: Decking with railings
description: Add decking platform option with configurable railings. Multiple railing styles (vertical slats, horizontal, glass, wire). Include in BOM.

title: More roof covering options
description: Expand roof materials: felt, shingles, EPDM, corrugated, tiles, green roof, etc. Visual + BOM implications.

title: Skylights/roof windows
description: Add skylights and roof windows. Position on roof slope, various sizes. Include flashing in BOM.

title: Soffit boards
description: Add soffit boards to roof overhangs. Visual finish detail + BOM.

title: Verify/debug BOM and cutting lists
description: Comprehensive audit of Bill of Materials and cutting list output. Check calculations, quantities, dimensions against real builds. Fix any discrepancies.

title: Price lists and supplier partnerships
description: Build price lists from real supplier costs. Explore partnerships with timber merchants, hardware suppliers. Enable accurate live pricing in configurator.

title: User activity tracking/analytics
description: Track when users log in, interact with models, save designs. Dashboard to see engagement. Helps identify serious enquiries vs browsers.

title: AI UI assistant
description: Introduce AI-powered UI for the configurator. Natural language input: 'I need a 3x4m workshop with double doors'. Voice or text interaction.

title: Simplified website version + 'Design Together' flow
description:
Two-tier approach:
1. Simplified configurator on website for self-service exploration
2. Invite to 'design together' session: 'I can come and we can design your building together'

Converts self-service browsers into consultative sales.

title: Paint and colour options
description:
Add colour/paint selection to configurator. Options could include:
• Exterior paint colours (similar to Crane's range)
• Wood stains/preservatives
• Farrow & Ball or premium options
• Two-tone (body + trim different colours)

Visual update in 3D model + include in BOM/quote. Note: Crane offers Exterior range, Farrow & Ball, and Sikkens stains.

title: Douglas Fir research
description:
Research on Douglas fir timber properties, comparison to competitors (Scandinavian Redwood), untreated vs treated, and marketing angles.

**File:** /research-douglas-fir.md

**Key finding:** Douglas fir is stronger, harder, and stiffer than the Scandinavian Redwood competitors use. Andrew's preference for untreated is valid — better workability, customer can choose finish.

title: "Why Douglas Fir?" website content
description:
Create content explaining Douglas fir choice:
• Stronger, harder, stiffer than standard redwood
• Preferred by craftsmen for workability
• Untreated by default (nicer to work with, customer choice on finish)
• Ages beautifully to silver-grey
• Genuine quality differentiator vs competitors

Could be website page and/or blog post.

title: Attachment apex roof refinements
description:
Improvements to attachment building apex roof:
• Cripple studs (king posts) - refine positioning
• Ridge beam/purlins - review and align with primary building
• General apex roof quality parity with main building

Not urgent - parked for later. Current implementation functional.

title: Attachment fascia board refinements
description:
Further improvements to fascia boards on attachment apex roof:
• Eaves fascia / barge board joint alignment
• Ensure clean meeting at corners
• Review positioning tolerances

Current implementation works but has room for polish.

title: Marketing Strategy document
description:
Consolidated marketing strategy bringing together all insights: brand story, differentiators, target customer, competitive position, messaging pillars, content strategy, SEO, sales funnel, social proof needs, pricing strategy, and action items.

**File:** /marketing-strategy.md

**References:**
• /competitor-analysis.md
• /competitor-crane-deep-dive.md
• /research-douglas-fir.md
• /memory/seo-analysis-bespokeshedcompany.md

title: Attachment apex gable end cladding infill
description: Revisit the gable end cladding for attachment buildings with apex roofs. Currently the triangular gable area may need better infill/trimming.

title: 🎬 Animation Production Pipeline (ongoing)
description:
Template system for producing professional animated videos from the configurator. Enables rapid visual demos for clients with minimal manual input.

**Goal:** Andrew describes what he wants in natural language → James produces production-ready video.

**Components:**
• Keyframe template (JSON format)
• Render script (captures frames via CDP)
• Shot library (reusable camera moves)

**Files:**
• /keyframes-v6.json (current keyframe data)
• /render-v8.js (capture script)
• /keyframe-editor.html (browser-based editor)
• /DEVELOPER-UPDATE-QUESTIONNAIRE.md

title: Document keyframe JSON format
description:
Create proper documentation for the keyframe JSON schema:
• frame (number) - frame index
• camera (string) - "alpha, beta, radius"
• note (string|null) - description
• preset (object|null) - full shed-designer-v1 preset

Document best practices for timing, spacing, transitions.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Configurable render script
description:
Make render script accept parameters:
• Model URL (not hardcoded)
• Output folder
• FPS (currently 12)
• WebSocket endpoint
• Preset delay timing

Enable deploying on any model variant without code changes.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Shot library - reusable camera sequences
description:
Build library of named camera moves that can be referenced by name:
• orbit - 360° rotation around model
• door-focus - zoom to door, pause, zoom out
• roof-sweep - pan across roof showing structure
• approach - start far, end close
• explode - decladding sequence
• walkthrough - full feature tour

Allows Andrew to say "do an orbit and door-focus" and James knows exactly what that means.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Detect model-ready state (reduce delays)
description:
Currently using fixed 4s delay after preset imports because model rebuild time is unpredictable. Investigate detecting when model is actually ready:
• Poll for mesh count stabilization
• Use scene.executeWhenReady()
• Listen for rebuild-complete events

Would significantly speed up capture process.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Natural language → animation mapping
description:
Build understanding of Andrew's shorthand so James can interpret requests like:
• "log store" = attachment on left
• "walkthrough" = specific shot sequence
• "show off the door detail" = door-focus shot
• "nice orbit" = smooth 360° rotation

Learned over time through collaboration. Document mappings as they emerge.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Keyframe editor improvements
description:
Enhance the browser-based keyframe editor:
• ✅ Editable frame numbers in list
• Visual timeline representation
• Preview camera positions
• Drag-and-drop reordering
• Import presets from configurator clipboard

**File:** /keyframe-editor.html

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: v8 animation capture complete
description:
Successfully captured 65-frame animation with proper rendering (no wireframe flicker).

**Sequence:**
• Frames 1-40: Zoom in
• Frames 41-53: Door widening (1000→1350mm, 2-frame gaps)
• Frame 55: Style change to double M&T
• Frames 55-65: Door close + zoom out

**Fix applied:** 4s delay after preset imports + forced render cycles.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Visual timeline editor in configurator
description:
In-browser timeline UI for building animations visually:

**Features:**
• Timeline bar at bottom of configurator
• Scrub playhead to preview any point in animation
• Click to add keyframe (captures camera + model state)
• Drag keyframes to retime
• Click keyframe to edit
• Real-time preview playback
• Export to JSON for render script

**Benefits:**
• Andrew can build sequences visually without manual frame numbers
• Iterate timing without James in the loop
• See the animation as you build it

**Complexity:** Medium-high. Needs Babylon camera hooks + preset system integration.

**Parent:** Animation Production Pipeline (#77)

title: Back gable insulation positioning fix
description:
Fixed back gable insulation panel positioning. For back gable, interior faces toward -Z, so FRONT face of insulation (at z_mm) should be flush with inner face of back gable.

**Commit:** ab398a6

title: Hide 'At Eaves' tie beam for insulated apex
description:
Insulated apex roofs require raised tie beam for roof insulation to work. UI now hides and disables 'At Eaves' option when variant=insulated AND roof=apex. Auto-forces 'raised' if 'eaves' was previously selected. Shows option again when switching back to Basic.

**Commits:** 0614077, 059f66f
**Verified:** Browser DOM check confirmed display:none + disabled:true

title: Review animation pipeline architecture
description:
Design review: how voice-to-preset, camera tracks, keyframe editor, and visual preview all connect.

**Components:**
• Voice-to-preset: Natural language → JSON preset → share URL
• Camera tracks: Reusable camera movements (orbit, zoom, approach)
• Keyframe editor: Browser tool for building sequences
• Visual preview: Timeline slider, thumbnail strips, frame verification

**Goal:** Sketch architecture diagram showing data flow from voice input to rendered video.

**See also:** Mind map updated with Animation Pipeline node cluster.

title: Design sub-agent prompts: Hygiene + Strategy
description:
Create prompt specs for two specialized sub-agents:

**Hygiene Agent**
- Reviews memory files periodically
- Identifies clutter and outdated info
- Proposes consolidations and archives
- Reports recommendations for approval

**Strategy Agent**
- Reviews capability landscape (mind map, tools, recent work)
- Looks for cross-pollination opportunities
- Proposes new combinations and experiments
- "What if we connected X to Y?" thinking

**Design considerations:**
- Clear scope and boundaries
- Access to right files
- Report format
- Approval workflow
- Run frequency (heartbeat? scheduled?)

title: Voice-to-voice chat with James
description:
Real-time voice conversation capability. Speak → James hears → James responds with voice.

**Prototype built:** /voice-chat/
- Browser-based speech recognition
- Routes through Clawdbot CLI
- TTS responses

**Issues encountered:**
- Cloudflare quick tunnels are ephemeral (new URL each time)
- Transcript accumulation bug (fixed)
- Server/tunnel die when sandbox restarts

**For proper implementation:**
- Persistent tunnel (Cloudflare account) or real domain
- Keep server running reliably
- Consider WebRTC for lower latency
- Mobile-friendly UI improvements

title: YouTube video summarization
description:
James can now fetch YouTube transcripts and summarize videos.

**Script:** /scripts/youtube-summarize.py
**Usage:** Send YouTube link + ask for summary
**Supports:** Manual captions, auto-generated, translation from other languages

title: Concrete pier foundation system
description: Dense concrete blocks on Type 1 MOT base. Stronger than timber-on-ground, never rots. Include in BOM: blocks, MOT quantity, mortar.

title: Floor frame bearers and joists
description: 5x2 or 6x2 treated timber frame on piers. Bearers run length, joists at 400mm centres. Include L-bracket fixings in BOM.

title: Anti-racking diagonal braces
description: Diagonal braces for walls without OSB sheathing. Runs corner-to-corner on inside face. Critical for structural integrity of shed-style builds.

title: Noggins / horizontal blocking
description: Horizontal braces between studs at mid-wall height. Strengthens wall, provides fixing points. Standard practice in UK shed building.

title: King studs and trimmers around openings
description: Doubled-up framing around doors/windows. King stud runs full height, trimmer bears the header. Standard structural framing practice.

title: Header beams over openings
description: Load-bearing timber spanning door/window openings. Transfers load to king studs. Critical for structural integrity.

title: Roof pitch calculator
description: Input pitch in degrees, auto-calculate rafter length and cuts. Use formula or app logic (Essential Roofing App style). Display pitch angle in UI.

title: Roof overhang / eaves parameter
description: Adjustable rafter overhang beyond wall line. Affects fascia position, soffit requirement, and weather protection. Common values: 0-300mm.

title: Ridge beam as separate element
description: Distinct ridge beam running length of building. Rafters meet at ridge. Include in BOM separately from rafters.

title: OSB roof deck / sheathing
description: 18mm T&G OSB on rafters before roofing membrane. Half-bond joints for strength. Include in BOM with sheet count.

title: Cladding cover calculation
description: Account for tongue/groove overlap when calculating cladding quantity. Cover ≠ board width. Critical for accurate BOM.

title: Shiplap cladding detail
description: Tongue & groove with rebate visualization. Show overlap joint detail. Different from current flat panel representation.

title: Corner posts / trim pieces
description: 22x22mm corner posts where cladding meets. Covers end grain, gives neat finish. Include in BOM.

title: Window and door trim pieces
description: Finishing trim around openings. Covers cladding edges, gives professional look. Various profile options.

title: Featheredge cladding option
description: Alternative to shiplap - overlapping wedge-profile boards. Traditional look, different BOM calculation.

title: Soffit boards
description: Underside of roof overhang. Closes gap between fascia and wall. Various widths depending on overhang.

title: Bargeboards on gable ends
description: Trim boards following roof pitch on gable ends. Finishes roof edge, protects rafter ends.

title: Synthetic slate tile roof covering
description:
Full layer stack for slate tile roofs (Tapco-style synthetic slate):

**Completed layers:**
• Breathable membrane (light blue, 0.5mm) - sits on OSB
• Tile battens (25x38mm treated timber, 143mm spacing)
• Ridge battens (25mm from ridge line, both slopes)
• Eaves battens (25mm from eaves edge)
• Slate tile surface (pewter grey, 5mm thick)

**Still to do:**
• Ridge tiles (V-profile cap along ridge)
• Texture/normal map for realistic appearance
• Visibility toggles for construction breakdown

**Files:**
• docs/src/elements/roof-tiles.js

**Branch:** feature/slate-tile-roof @ b3bc44d

title: Internal wall cladding options
description:
Add dropdown for internal wall finish options:
• Plywood (current default for insulated)
• Tongue & groove (8mm planed)
• Painted/primed options

Visual update in 3D + include in BOM. Requested by customer enquiry for fully insulated build.

title: Relative door/window positioning
description:
When building dimensions change, doors and windows should maintain their relative position on the wall (percentage-based) rather than absolute mm position.

Example: A centered door stays centered when wall length changes, instead of migrating to one side.

Similar to how door width changes expand from center.

title: Door hinge graphics look wrong
description: The 3D representation of door hinges looks weird/unrealistic. Review hinge geometry and improve visual appearance. May need better positioning, scaling, or mesh detail.

title: Hipped roof insulation + ply liner not working
description: Insulation and plywood liner for hipped roof variant isn't working properly. Needs investigation — may be geometry positioning, visibility, or build logic specific to hipped roof style.

title: Attachment building polish: visibility checkboxes + insulation/ply organisation
description:
Attached buildings have good progress but need finishing touches:
1. Visibility checkboxes not working properly for attachment elements
2. Insulation and ply liner layers need better organisation in the attachment context

Low priority - park for now, revisit later.

title: Fix share URL missing state fields
description: Share URLs were missing roof covering, tie beam, cladding style, and attachment roof visibility fields. Added them. Commit 7b9d077.

title: Fix attachment height capping 168mm too low
description: Eaves heights should be from floor surface not ground level. Was 168mm short. Commit cea3d74.

title: Preset overhaul: new presets, categories, cleanup
description: Updated hipped garage, added Apex Garden Room + Small Pent Shed, removed debug/old presets, fixed hipped membrane/battens. 10 commits pushed.

title: Pent insulation + ply follows roof slope
description: Wall insulation and ply lining on pent roofs now follows the slope per-bay using vertex modification (not CSG). Per-wall heights for left/right walls. Multiple iterations. Commits d33558d → dfc865e.

title: Fix pent plyboard ghost wireframe above frame
description: enableEdgesRendering() was called before vertex slope adjustment, causing ghost outline at original box shape. Fix: moved to after vertex mod, added updatable:true + refreshBoundingInfo(). Commit d778f6f.

title: Fix pent roof plateThickness in floor stack calculation
description: plateThickness was incorrectly included in FLOOR_STACK_MM in roof.js — it's part of the wall frame, not the floor. Removed to align with walls.js pentStackAdjust. Commit 13c0aa3.

title: Pricing engine: per-component costing, margins, suppliers, VAT
description:
Full pricing system for the configurator BOM:
- Cost price input per component
- Multiple cost options per component (e.g. different grades/sizes)
- Multiple suppliers per component (compare pricing)
- Programmable margin percentages per component category
- VAT toggle (show with/without)
- Needs careful design thinking before implementation

Part of UI polish phase.

title: Fix memory_search — add embeddings API key
description: memory_search is failing for all agents due to missing OpenAI/Google embeddings API key in auth-profiles.json. Only Anthropic key configured. Need to run: clawdbot agents auth main --provider openai --token sk-xxx (or Google). Semantic search across memory files is broken — workaround is manual file reads.

title: Seamless voice interaction with James
description: High-quality, reliable voice calling between Andrew and James. Current state: Tailscale funnel + Clawdbot dashboard voice exists but setup is fragile (bind issues, DNS, auth). Goal: one-tap voice call from phone, reliable connection, natural conversation quality. Investigate: LiveKit, WebRTC, Clawdbot native voice, persistent tunnel setup.

title: Budget buildings range — mushroom/microgreen grow sheds
description: Research and develop cheapest possible insulated shed range designed as controlled environments for growing mushrooms and microgreens. Includes: supplier research, material costs, insulation options, ventilation/humidity requirements, pricing.

title: Budget cladding render issues & colour logic
description: Box profile/corrugated steel cladding renders too thin/transparent. Colour picker logic needs verification — materials not applying visibly. Also check galvanised trim, corner flashings, and fascia material for budget styles. Infinite loop crash fixed (CLAD_H=0) but visual quality needs work.

title: Building Type Selector — Phase 1 (Gazebo)
description: Add building type dropdown replacing 'Design Your Shed' title. First type: Gazebo — 4 corner posts (150x150), hipped roof, no walls/cladding. Store buildingType in state, hide irrelevant controls.

title: Building Type Selector — Phase 2 (Full Range)
description: Extend building type dropdown with: Summer House, Garden Room, Garage, Workshop, Lean-to, Field Shelter. Each type defines its own defaults, visible controls, and rendering rules.

title: Photo paste/attach support in dashboards
description: Paste or attach photos into dashboard cards/ideas. PRIMARY USE: mobile phone on-site. Must be visible to ALL users (not localStorage). UX: tap button → camera/gallery picker (input accept=image/*), compress before upload (phone photos are huge), then commit to data/images/ via GitHub API. Desktop: also support paste/drag-drop. Apply across all dashboards.

title: Whelpley Farm: mobile first-load is blank/zoomed out
description: On mobile, dashboard loads looking like a blank screen — have to zoom and pan to find the content. Needs to load zoomed in on the dashboard features. Check viewport meta, sidebar collapse on mobile, initial scroll position. First impression matters.

title: Mobile dashboard UX: split-screen like configurator
description: All dashboards on mobile are tough to use with the sidebar-wizard pattern. Apply the configurator split-screen approach: content top, navigation bottom, drag handle. Consistent mobile UX across all dashboards (Forge AI, Whelpley Farm, GrowCabin, Command Center, Shed Board). Major UX unification piece.

title: Work sizing: Projects / Jobs / Tasks hierarchy
description: Introduce 3-level sizing across all dashboards. Task = ≤2 hours (single action). Job = ~1 day (group of tasks). Project = multiple days (group of jobs). Add as a field to cards. Enables better time estimation, prioritisation, and filtering. Could nest Jobs under Projects and Tasks under Jobs.

title: Mobile: no building type selector (can't access gazebo)
description: buildingTypeSelect dropdown is only in sidebar-wizard.js (desktop). Mobile configurator never creates it, so users can't switch building type on mobile. Fix: add building type selector to mobile layout, probably at top of Size step. Also needs to trigger updateBuildingTypeUI() and hide irrelevant steps for gazebo.

title: Whelpley Farm: Ideas sync across devices
description: Ideas stored in data/ideas.json in repo, synced via GitHub API. PAT entered once, stored in localStorage. Merge logic: remote wins conflicts, local adds new offline ideas.

title: AI Transition Coaching Business (Forge AI)
description: Started as a business idea in the configurator incubator. Now graduated to its own project with dashboard, branding (Ember Palette), and website planning underway. Archived from garden buildings Ideas.

title: 🚀 Landing Pages — Vercel Deployment
description: Deploy 3 SEO-targeted local landing pages on Vercel. Pages funnel visitors into the configurator. Priority from ChatGPT Week 1 roadmap (16 Feb). Covers: domain setup, Vercel project, deployment pipeline.

title: 🌍 Landing Page 1: Garden Buildings Salisbury
description: Local SEO landing page targeting "bespoke garden buildings Salisbury" and related keywords. Includes configurator embed/link, testimonials placeholder, service area, contact CTA.

title: 🌍 Landing Page 2: Garden Offices Wiltshire
description: Local SEO landing page targeting "garden offices Wiltshire" and related keywords. Configurator link, benefits of bespoke vs prefab, pricing transparency via configurator.

title: 🌍 Landing Page 3: Custom Sheds Hampshire
description: Local SEO landing page targeting "custom sheds Hampshire" and related keywords. Showcases range of building types, links to configurator for instant design.

title: 🔗 Lead Capture Integration
description: Add lead capture to configurator and landing pages. When customer designs a shed and wants a quote, capture email/phone + configurator state URL. Feeds into pipeline tracking.

title: 📊 Domain & Vercel Setup
description: Connect bespokeshedcompany.co.uk domain to Vercel. Set up project, deployment from GitHub, DNS configuration.

title: 🔍 SEO Keyword Research — Local Terms
description: Research best local keyword targets for the 3 landing pages. Validate search volume for Salisbury/Wiltshire/Hampshire garden building terms. Feeds into page copy and meta tags.

title: Custom domain for share links (Edge compatibility)
description: Edge blocks .workers.dev domains (ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED). Add a custom domain to the Cloudflare Worker (e.g. share.andrewsgparsons.com) so share links work in all browsers including Edge. Many customers are retired and use Edge as default. Steps: 1) Add custom domain route in Cloudflare Workers dashboard, 2) Update WORKER_URL in share-link.js, 3) Test in Edge.
